steps.title: Use the equal legs
steps.content: The figure shows a right triangle with two congruent legs, each of length $32$. A right triangle with congruent legs is isosceles, so its two acute angles are congruent.

steps.title: Use the triangle angle sum
steps.content: The right angle measures $90^{\circ}$, leaving $90^{\circ}$ for the two equal acute angles. Therefore, $$2x=90,$$ so $$x=45.$$
